docs.title: nParse+
docs.text:
An EverQuest Project 1999 companion overlay for macOS, Windows, and Linux.

nParse+ tails your EQ log file and gives you, in real time: per-target spell and buff timers, a full trigger engine with voice alerts, a DPS meter, live maps with an NPC finder, respawn timers, complete-heal chain tracking, raid-AOE countdowns, and mob info with live loot pricing.

It reads the log file only — no memory reading, no injection, no game files modified (the optional Night Vision fix is applied only when you ask, with backups).

The nParse+ overlays over the game — screenshot pending (home--overview.png)

docs.title: Standing on the shoulders of giants
docs.text:
nParse+ exists because of three excellent tools that came before it, and it gladly acknowledges its debt to each:

  • nParse by nomns — the original cross-platform P99 parser this project is forked from (GPL-3.0). The map overlay heritage and the self-hostable location sharing come from here.
  • EQTool / PigParse by smasherprog and contributors (MIT) — the Windows tool whose feature set, trigger library, zone/respawn databases, and network protocol nParse+ ports 1:1. nParse+ interoperates live with EQTool users on the PigParse network.
  • GINA by Gimagukk — the archetypal EQ trigger tool whose trigger/overlay/TTS model shaped what players expect from any parser.

See Why nParse+ for how the feature sets compare, and Migrating if you're coming from one of them.

docs.title: What you get
docs.text: Area What you get Spell timers Per-target buff/debuff countdowns from the real spell DB (durations by your level/class), gem icons, per-class spell filters, worn-off removal, cooldowns, self-buffs restored across camps Triggers 65 built-in triggers (raid AOEs with countdown bars, invis/levitate fading, failed feign, charm break, death touch, …) plus custom triggers with {name}/{c}/{COUNTER} tokens, zone gating, text-to-speech, timers, and counters. Start ad-hoc timers from chat: StartTimer-45-Label Maps Brewall map set, player marker with direction arrow via /loc, tracking-skill radius circle (Druid/Ranger/Bard), smooth z-axis fading tuned per zone, spawn-point timers, waypoints, path recording, and NPC search with live P99-wiki lookup DPS meter Per-fight attacker breakdown, 12-second trailing DPS, session best/current/last Combat tracking Respawn timers on kill (per-NPC times for 121 zones), faction/exp kill guessing, random-roll tracking, quake/Ring War/FTE alerts, death-loop detection, pet tracking CH chains CA 001 CH -- Target calls render as chips sliding across an 11-second lane per heal target; voice and on-screen warning when your slot is next Event overlay Full-screen click-through alerts and countdown bars; position it over your game window from the tray Mob info /consider a mob → respawn time, notable flag, known loot with live auction prices, one-click wiki page PigParse network Live interop with EQTool users: shared player dots on the map, shared Kael pull timers and dragon roars, quake/boat/roll-timer feeds, a shared /who roster, pigparse.org Discord login with inventory upload — plus a self-hostable nparse websocket mode Night Vision fix One click applies the community shader/sky fix over your EQ install (with backups) — and one click reverts it Friends sync Merge every character's in-game friends list on a server and push it back to all of them (originals backed up) Boats Boat schedule tracking synced from sightings, yours or shared over the network

docs.title: Why nParse+
docs.text:
nParse+ set out to give every platform what Windows players get from EQTool/PigParse — and to fold in the best of nParse and the trigger workflow GINA made standard. This page shows where it stands next to those tools.

First, the debt

None of this is a knock on the tools below — nParse+ literally is nParse (GPL-3.0 fork lineage) plus a 1:1 port of EQTool's feature set and data (MIT, pinned source commit in CREDITS.md), with GINA as the archetype for how trigger tools should feel. If you're happy on one of them, keep using it — nParse+ interoperates with EQTool users on the network either way.

docs.title: Feature matrix
docs.text:
Legend: ✅ full support · 🟡 partial / different approach · ❌ not available

Feature nParse+ EQTool nParse GINA Platforms ✅ macOS, Windows, Linux ❌ Windows only ✅ cross-platform ❌ Windows only Spell/buff timers (real spell DB, level-scaled) ✅ ✅ 🟡 casting-based guesses ❌ Custom triggers (text + TTS + timers + counters) ✅ ✅ 🟡 simple custom timers ✅ Built-in raid trigger library ✅ (ported from EQTool) ✅ ❌ ❌ (community shares packages) Trigger tokens ({name}, {c}, {COUNTER}) ✅ ✅ ❌ ✅ ({S}, {C} style) Zone-gated triggers ✅ ✅ ❌ ❌ Maps with live position ✅ ✅ ✅ ❌ NPC search + live P99 wiki lookup ✅ ✅ ❌ ❌ Spawn-point / respawn timers (per-NPC times) ✅ ✅ 🟡 manual spawn timers ❌ DPS meter ✅ ✅ ❌ ❌ CH chain tracking ✅ ✅ ❌ ❌ Mob info on /consider (respawn, loot, prices) ✅ ✅ ❌ ❌ PigParse network (shared map dots, timers, feeds) ✅ live interop ✅ ❌ ❌ Self-hostable location sharing ✅ (nparse websocket mode) ❌ ✅ ❌ Death-loop detection ✅ ✅ ❌ ❌ Boat schedules ✅ ✅ ❌ ❌ Night Vision fix (apply/revert) ✅ ✅ ❌ ❌ Friends-list sync across characters ✅ ✅ ❌ ❌ Per-character profiles ✅ ✅ ❌ ✅ (per-character trigger sets) Text-to-speech alerts ✅ native on all 3 OSes ✅ ❌ ✅ Discord overlay ✅ ❌ ✅ ❌ Self-updater ✅ ✅ ❌ ✅ Trigger export/import (shareable files) ✅ JSON packs ❌ ❌ ✅ (.gtp packages) Trigger import from GINA ✅ reads .gtp/XML (details) 🟡 ❌ —

docs.title: What only nParse+ offers
docs.text:
• The EQTool feature set outside Windows. Everything in the EQTool column above, running natively on macOS and Linux (including a Flatpak with flatpak update support) as well as Windows.
  • Live PigParse interop from any OS — your map dots, shared timers, and feeds appear to EQTool users and theirs to you, from a Mac or Linux box.
  • Both sharing networks in one tool: the PigParse hub and nParse's self-hostable websocket mode for private groups.
  • One unified settings window with per-character profiles, per-window opacity/always-on-top/click-through, spell class filters, and window layout presets.
  • nParse's map + Discord overlay heritage combined with EQTool's spell/trigger/combat engine — previously you had to pick one.

docs.title: What the others still do better
docs.text:
Honesty cuts both ways:

  • GINA has a huge ecosystem of shared trigger packages. nParse+ now imports .gtp packages directly (sound files and phrase modifiers aside) and has its own JSON export/import, but the community library itself still lives in GINA's world.
  • EQTool is the reference implementation of the PigParse network and gets its own features first; nParse+ ports them after.
  • nParse (upstream) is smaller and simpler if all you want is the map.

If you're coming from any of these, the Migrating pages map each tool's concepts to their nParse+ equivalents.

docs.title: Does nParse+ read game memory or modify the game?
docs.text: No. nParse+ works by reading the log file the game itself writes — the same file you can open in a text editor. It never reads game memory, injects anything, or touches game files in normal operation. The one opt-in exception is the Night Vision fix, which replaces shader/texture files only when you click Apply (with backups and a Revert button). Whether any third-party tool is permitted is always the server's call — check your server's current rules.

docs.title: Which servers does it work with?
docs.text: It's built for Project 1999 (Green, Blue, Red) — the zone, respawn, and spell databases are P99-era. The parsing itself works with any classic-era EQ client that writes standard log lines.

docs.title: Does it work with WINE / CrossOver / Whisky / Lutris?
docs.text: Yes — that's the main reason it exists. nParse+ runs natively on your Mac or Linux desktop and just reads the log file inside the wrapper's filesystem. See First run for where the Logs folder hides per setup.

docs.title: Can I import my GINA triggers?
docs.text: Not yet — there's no importer. See Migrating from GINA for the concept map and a fast manual workflow; the built-in triggers already cover the standard raid alerts.

docs.title: Do I need an account for the shared map dots?
docs.text: No. The PigParse network features — map dots, shared timers, feeds — work without any account. The optional Discord login only adds inventory upload to your pigparse.org character page.

docs.title: Can other people see where I am?
docs.text: Only if you turn sharing on, and you control it per character: everyone / guild-only / off (Settings → Character). With sharing off nothing leaves your machine. Locations are only sent when you type /loc.

docs.title: Why does macOS say the app is damaged?
docs.text: The app isn't notarized (that needs a paid Apple developer account), so macOS quarantines the download. One command clears it — see Install on macOS.

docs.title: The overlays disappear when I click the game — why?
docs.text: EQ is probably running in exclusive fullscreen. Overlays can only draw over a windowed or borderless game. This applies on every platform.

docs.title: My spell timer durations look wrong.
docs.text: Set your class and level in Settings → Character — durations scale with both. Also consider pointing EQ install directory (Settings → General) at your install so nParse+ reads your actual spells_us.txt.

docs.title: Does it work with multiple characters / two boxes?
docs.text: Per-character profiles are automatic — nParse+ follows the newest log file, so camping to another character switches everything over. It watches one log at a time, though: for true two-boxing you'd run two instances with separate settings.

docs.title: My log files are getting huge.
docs.text: The game only ever appends. Turn on Archive oversized logs in Settings → Advanced.

docs.title: Something's broken — where do I look?
docs.text: Troubleshooting, and the two log files listed there. Bug reports: GitHub issues.

docs.title: Roadmap
docs.text:
Where nParse+ is headed. No dates promised — this is a hobby project — but these are the features actively planned, roughly in the order they're likely to land. Want something moved up (or something new)? Open an issue.

Each item below is tracked as a GitHub issue (labelled by area / type / size) — the issues are the live status; this page is the prose overview.

docs.title: Distribution & platform
docs.text:
Longer-horizon packaging work, waiting on time (and in some cases, money):

  • macOS notarization — removes the xattr step
  • Windows code signing + installer — removes the SmartScreen warning
  • Flathub submission — flatpak install flathub … instead of sideloading (the self-hosted repo already gives flatpak update)
  • Delta updates for the standalone builds — the Flatpak already updates incrementally; signed patch updates for the DMG/zip/tarball are planned
  • 3D map view

docs.title: Shipped
docs.text: Everything that used to be on this list and made it: see the changelog and the releases page.

docs.title: First: the two log files
docs.text:
Since v1.4.1 nParse+ writes its own diagnostics — check these before anything else, and attach them to bug reports:

File Contents crash.log Unhandled-error tracebacks nparseplus.log Warnings and info — sharing reconnects, update checks, applied defaults

Where they live:

Platform Location macOS ~/Library/Logs/nparseplus/ Windows %LOCALAPPDATA%\nparseplus\Logs\ Linux (tarball/source) ~/.local/state/nparseplus/log/ Linux (Flatpak) ~/.var/app/io.github.prokopto_dev.nparse_plus/.local/state/nparseplus/log/

docs.title: Nothing is parsing / windows never update
docs.text:
1. Is logging on in game? Type /log on (it persists, but check).
  2. Is the EQ Logs directory right? (Settings → General) It must be the folder containing eqlog_<Name>_<server>.txt files.
  3. Open the Console and say something in game — if it appears, parsing is fine and the issue is elsewhere.
  4. Remember nParse+ follows the newest log file — a stray recently -touched eqlog_ file from another character can steal the tail.

docs.title: Overlays vanish behind the game, or won't stay on top
docs.text:
• Run EQ windowed or borderless, never exclusive fullscreen (all platforms).
  • Check the window's On top flag in Settings → Windows.
  • On Linux, nParse+ must run through X11/XWayland (it does this by default) — don't force QT_QPA_PLATFORM=wayland; native Wayland windows cannot stay on top or remember positions.

docs.title: A window is stuck somewhere / can't be clicked
docs.text:
• Click-through on? You can't grab it with the mouse — turn click-through off in Settings → Windows.
  • Off-screen (e.g. after unplugging a monitor)? Apply any saved layout from tray → Window Layouts, or toggle the window off and on.

docs.title: macOS
docs.text:
• "App is damaged / unverified developer" — the quarantine flag; run the xattr command. Needed again after each update.
  • No tray icon? It's in the menu bar (top right), not the Dock.

docs.title: Windows
docs.text: • SmartScreen blocks the exe — More info → Run anyway (details).

docs.title: Linux
docs.text:
• No tray icon on GNOME — install the AppIndicator extension.
  • Flatpak can't see the EQ folder — it's outside $HOME; grant access with flatpak override (guide).
  • Discord overlay crashes/blank — the Chromium sandbox issue; see the tarball notes.

docs.title: Sharing won't connect / no dots
docs.text:
1. Sharing mode set? (Settings → Sharing) — and restart after changing it.
  2. This character's location sharing on? (Settings → Character)
  3. You only receive a zone's players after sending a location — type /loc in game.
  4. Check nparseplus.log for reconnect reasons (paths above); the tray menu's top line shows live sharing status.

docs.title: Spell timers are wrong or missing
docs.text:
• Class/level not set → durations can't scale — type /who in game and they auto-fill (Settings → Character).
  • "Show only your own spells" hides other players' casts (Settings → Spell Timers).
  • A whole section missing (Boats / Custom Timer / Timers / rolls)? Its Show … toggle in Settings → Spell Timers may be off — or someone right-clicked and cleared it (cleared timers restart on their next trigger; hidden ones come back instantly when re-enabled).
  • Ambiguous cast messages need Guess ambiguous spells on to show a best-guess row.

docs.title: Settings seem lost / where is settings.json?
docs.text:
See First run → Where settings live. Note the Flatpak keeps its own copy under ~/.var/app/… — a tarball install and a Flatpak install have separate settings.

Still stuck? Open a GitHub issue with your platform, the app version (tray menu, top entry), and the two log files.

docs.title: Development
docs.text: nParse+ is GPL-3.0, written in Python 3.12 with PySide6, and developed at github.com/prokopto-dev/nparse-plus.

docs.title: Running from source
docs.text:
Requires uv (which installs Python 3.12+ for you):

git clone https://github.com/prokopto-dev/nparse-plus.git nparseplus && cd nparseplus
uv sync
uv run python -m nparseplus

docs.title: Development commands
docs.text:
uv run pytest                             # ~770 tests, a few seconds
uv run pytest --cov=nparseplus --cov-branch --cov-report=term-missing
uv run ruff check . && uv run ruff format .
QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen uv run pytest   # headless (CI does this)
uv run mkdocs serve                       # preview this documentation

docs.title: Architecture in one paragraph
docs.text: A log driver tails the newest eqlog_*.txt, a parser chain (one module per EQTool parser, 1:1 ports) turns lines into typed events on an event bus, handlers mutate services (timers, fights, triggers), and the Qt UI reads snapshots on timers. The rule that matters: the core engine never imports Qt — a test enforces it. The full guide lives in CLAUDE.md in the repo; EQTool's C# (pinned commit in CREDITS.md) is the source of truth for ported behavior, and its EQtoolsTests corpus is the golden spec.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text:
• Commits follow Conventional Commits — feat: / fix: determine release versions (see Release flow).
  • Ported parser/handler behavior should match EQTool exactly; port the C# test first when in doubt. Deliberate divergences get a comment.
  • Data files under data/ are generated by tools/convert_*.py — regenerate, don't hand-edit.

docs.title: Roadmap
docs.text: Planned features and the distribution parking lot live on the Roadmap page; shipped history is in the changelog.

docs.title: Building packages
docs.text: All release artifacts are PyInstaller onedir builds, wrapped per platform. CI builds everything on tag push (release flow); locally:

docs.title: macOS (.app / DMG)
docs.text:
uv sync --group build
uv run pyinstaller packaging/nparseplus.spec --noconfirm   # dist/nParse+.app
codesign --force --deep -s - "dist/nParse+.app"            # ad-hoc sign
uv run dmgbuild -s packaging/dmg_settings.py "nParse+" dist/nParse+.dmg

docs.title: Windows (zip)
docs.text:
uv sync --group build
uv run pyinstaller packaging/nparseplus.spec --noconfirm
Compress-Archive -Path dist/nparseplus -DestinationPath dist/nparseplus-win64.zip

docs.title: Linux (tarball + Flatpak)
docs.text:
uv sync --group build
uv run pyinstaller packaging/nparseplus.spec --noconfirm
tar -C dist -czf nparseplus-linux-x86_64.tar.gz nparseplus

The Flatpak wraps the same onedir build via the manifest in packaging/flatpak/ — building it needs a Linux box with flatpak-builder (commands are in the manifest header; CI does this on release). Release builds are GPG-signed and published to an OSTree repo on GitHub Pages, which is what makes flatpak update work.

docs.title: Documentation site
docs.text:
uv sync --group docs
uv run mkdocs serve            # live preview at http://127.0.0.1:8000
uv run mkdocs build --strict   # the CI gate

Versioned deploys use mike; CI handles them (release flow). Screenshots go in docs/assets/screenshots/ — missing ones render as placeholders, and docs/dev-notes/screenshot-checklist.md lists what's wanted.

docs.title: Release flow
docs.text:
Releases are driven by Conventional Commits and Python Semantic Release:

  • fix: / perf: → patch release
  • feat: → minor release
  • feat!: / BREAKING CHANGE: → major release
  • chore: / ci: / docs: etc. → no release

docs.title: The pipeline
docs.text:
1. Semantic Release workflow (manual dispatch, or uv run semantic-release version locally): runs the ruff+pytest gate, computes the next version from the commit log, bumps pyproject.toml and nparseplus.__version__, updates CHANGELOG.md, commits, tags v<X.Y.Z>, and dispatches the package workflow (tags created with GITHUB_TOKEN don't trigger workflows on their own).
  2. Release workflow (release.yml) verifies the tag matches both version files, then builds in parallel:
  3. macOS DMG (ad-hoc signed)
  4. Windows zip
  5. Linux tarball and Flatpak bundle (GPG-signed; smoke-tested headless inside the sandbox)
  6. publishes the Flatpak OSTree repo to the gh-pages branch — preserving the deployed docs — so flatpak update works
  7. The release job collects the artifacts, extracts that version's changelog section, and publishes the GitHub release.
  8. The docs job deploys this documentation as version <X.Y> with the latest alias (via mike), from the tagged tree.

Between releases, pushes to master that touch docs/ redeploy the dev docs version automatically (docs-dev.yml).

docs.title: gh-pages layout
docs.text:
One branch serves both consumers:

gh-pages/
  repo/                      # Flatpak OSTree repo (URL must never move)
  nparseplus.flatpakref      # embed GPG key; flatpak install source
  nparseplus.flatpakrepo
  1.4/  dev/  latest/        # mike-managed docs versions
  versions.json  index.html  # mike: version list + redirect to latest

The Flatpak publish step rebuilds the branch as a single orphan commit each release (so OSTree objects never pile up in git history) but seeds it from the previous tree, so the docs directories survive. mike then commits its docs updates on top. Don't hand-edit gh-pages.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
The engine behind the windows: what nParse+ parses out of your log and what it does with it.

Alerts & timers

  • Triggers — the full trigger model: search text, regex, tokens, zone gating, outputs
  • Built-in triggers — the shipped library of ~65 raid/combat/utility triggers
  • Chat-command timers — StartTimer-45-Label from any chat channel
  • Respawn & zone timers — per-NPC respawn countdowns on kill
  • Text-to-speech — spoken alerts on every platform

Combat

  • CH chains — complete-heal chain parsing and your-slot warnings
  • Combat tracking — the DPS engine, death-loop detection, pets, random rolls, faction/exp guessing

World

  • Boats — boat schedules from sightings
  • Night Vision fix — the community shader/sky fix, applied and reverted safely

Network & accounts

  • Sharing & PigParse — map dots, shared timers, and feeds, interoperating with EQTool users; plus the self-hostable nparse mode
  • Friends sync — one merged friends list across your characters

App

  • Self-updater — release checks and per-platform updates

docs.title: Boats
docs.text: P99's boats run on fixed schedules, but knowing where in the cycle a boat is requires seeing it. nParse+ tracks boat sightings and projects the schedule forward.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
When a boat's arrival announcement appears in your log (or arrives over the PigParse network from another player who saw it), nParse+ anchors that boat's schedule: it knows the time from announcement to dock and the full round-trip time for each boat, so one sighting yields the next docking and departure times.

  • Sightings you make are shared to the network, and everyone else's sightings flow to you — so on a populated server the boat schedule is usually "just known" without anyone standing on the dock.
  • Boat timers appear as timer rows in the Boats section of Spell Timers, so you can see how long until the Sirensbane or the Maiden's Voyage comes back before you decide to swim. Landlubbers can hide the section with "Show boat timers" in Settings → Spell Timers.

This is a port of EQTool's boat service over the same shared feed, so nParse+ and EQTool users pool their sightings.

docs.title: Built-in triggers
docs.text:
nParse+ ships 67 ready-made triggers — 65 ported 1:1 from EQTool's built-in library (raid AOE countdowns, combat quality-of-life alerts, and utility reminders) plus two nParse+ utility additions that render in the event overlay's dedicated Utility header section.

They all start disabled. Open the Trigger Editor and check the ones (or whole folders) you want. Built-ins can be edited to taste — Revert restores the stock definition — but never deleted, so updates can keep them in sync.

The encounter triggers are zone-gated: a dragon-breath countdown only fires in its dragon's zone.
